jd_text: Job Description What if you could do the kind of work the world needs? At WSP, you can access our global scale, contribute to landmark projects and connect with the brightest minds in your field to do the best work of your life. You can embrace your curiosity in a culture that celebrates new ideas and diverse perspectives. You can experience a world of opportunity and the chance to shape a career as unique as you. As an Associate Director in Building Structures, you will provide strategic leadership and technical oversight for the structural engineering discipline across major and complex projects. You will be responsible for driving innovation, ensuring technical excellence, and managing client relationships at the highest level. This role involves shaping business strategy, leading multidisciplinary teams, and contributing to the growth and reputation of the organization. Responsibilities Lead the structural engineering function across multiple large-scale projects, ensuring delivery to the highest standards of quality, safety, and efficiency. Act as a trusted advisor to clients, providing strategic guidance and technical expertise on complex structural challenges. Define and implement technical strategies, design standards, and best practices for the discipline. Oversee resource planning, project budgets, and schedules to ensure successful delivery and profitability. Drive innovation in structural design, digital engineering, and sustainability initiatives. Mentor and develop senior leaders, engineers, and technical teams, fostering a culture of excellence and collaboration. Represent the company in high-level client meetings, industry forums, and conferences, contributing to thought leadership and brand positioning. Support business development through strategic proposals, bids, and client presentations, helping secure major projects. Ensure compliance with Health, Safety, and Environmental (HSE) standards and regulatory requirements across all projects. Maintain governance over quality assurance processes, risk management, and contractual obligations. Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Structural or Civil Engineering (Master’s preferred) Minimum 20+ years of experience in structural engineering, including leadership roles on iconic and complex building projects Proven track record in managing large teams and delivering high-value projects Expertise in steel and concrete design and advanced structural analysis In-depth knowledge of UAE, KSA, and international design codes Demonstrated ability to contribute to strategic growth and business development initiatives Imagine a better future for you and a better future for us all. Join our close-knit community of talented individuals who share your passion for making a positive impact. Our global team includes more than 69,000 employees, working together to make a difference in communities both close to home and around the world. With us, you can. Apply today.
